l1: Question 1 — When did the backup failures first occur?
Question 2 — Is the issue affecting full backups, incremental backups, or both?
Question 3 — Is the backup target local disk, external disk, or network share?
Question 4 — Are multiple servers affected or only one?
Question 5 — Did the issue start after an OS update or patch?
Question 6 — Is the backup schedule configured through Windows Server Backup or Task Scheduler?
Question 7 — Are users reporting restore failures or only backup failures?
Question 8 — Is the backup volume encrypted or compressed?
l2: Advanced question 1 — Are there Event Viewer logs showing backup errors (Event ID 517, 546, 565)?
Advanced question 2 — Is the backup target disk healthy and accessible?
Advanced question 3 — Is the backup configured for bare-metal recovery or file-level?
Advanced question 4 — Are VSS (Volume Shadow Copy Service) writers failing?
Advanced question 5 — Is the backup target part of clustered storage?
Advanced question 6 — Are there performance drops or timeouts during backup?
Advanced question 7 — Is deduplication enabled on the backup target?
Advanced question 8 — Are large files or databases failing to back up consistently?

l1: Command 1 — Open Windows Server Backup console and check job status
Command 2 — Run wbadmin get status to confirm backup state
Command 3 — Review Event Viewer → Applications and Services Logs → Microsoft → Windows → Backup
Command 4 — Verify backup target accessibility with File Explorer
Command 5 — Run wbadmin get versions to list available backups
Command 6 — Check disk space on backup target
Command 7 — Run vssadmin list writers to confirm VSS writer health
Command 8 — Validate Task Scheduler entries for backup jobs
l2: Advanced command 1 — Run wbadmin start backup with verbose logging
Advanced command 2 — Use vssadmin list shadows to check shadow copy creation
Advanced command 3 — Run Get-WBPolicy in PowerShell to review backup configuration
Advanced command 4 — Query cluster logs if backup is clustered
Advanced command 5 — Run wbadmin get items to confirm backup scope
Advanced command 6 — Review deduplication logs if backup target uses deduplication
Advanced command 7 — Run wbadmin start recovery to test restore process
Advanced command 8 — Use Performance Monitor counters for backup throughput

l1: Step 1 — Restart Windows Server Backup service
Step 2 — Verify backup target disk health and connectivity
Step 3 — Free up disk space on backup target
Step 4 — Run vssadmin list writers and restart services if writers failed
Step 5 — Reconfigure backup schedule in Windows Server Backup
Step 6 — Test manual backup with wbadmin start backup
Step 7 — Ensure backup target permissions are correct
Step 8 — Disable antivirus temporarily if interfering with backup
l2: Advanced fix 1 — Run wbadmin start backup with custom parameters to isolate issue
Advanced fix 2 — Repair VSS writers by restarting related services (COM+ Event System, VSS, etc.)
Advanced fix 3 — Replace or reformat corrupted backup target disk
Advanced fix 4 — Recreate backup policy with Get-WBPolicy and Set-WBPolicy
Advanced fix 5 — Validate cluster configuration if clustered backup is failing
Advanced fix 6 — Escalate to Microsoft support if VSS metadata corruption persists
Advanced fix 7 — Update storage controller firmware and drivers
Advanced fix 8 — Restore from alternate backup if primary backup is unrecoverable

l1: Collection 1 — Export Event Viewer logs from Applications and Services Logs → Microsoft → Windows → Backup
Collection 2 — Run wbadmin get status and export results
Collection 3 — Execute wbadmin get versions output and save
Collection 4 — Capture screenshot of Windows Server Backup console
Collection 5 — Collect OS build and patch level
Collection 6 — Export vssadmin list writers output
Collection 7 — Gather disk space and health report for backup target
Collection 8 — Save Task Scheduler XML for backup jobs
l2: Advanced collection 1 — Export wbadmin start backup verbose logs
Advanced collection 2 — Collect vssadmin list shadows output
Advanced collection 3 — Gather cluster logs if backup is clustered
Advanced collection 4 — Export Get-WBPolicy configuration
Advanced collection 5 — Collect deduplication logs if enabled
Advanced collection 6 — Gather Performance Monitor counters for backup throughput
Advanced collection 7 — Collect firmware/driver versions for storage controllers
Advanced collection 8 — Export wbadmin start recovery test logs

steps.expect: Backup job shows failed or incomplete
Errors related to backup job execution
One or more VSS writers show failed state
Insufficient space or disk errors identified

steps.action: Restart VSS and Windows Server Backup services
